2026-04-09T17:21:25.5055166Z   └ [{"content":[{"text":"successfully downloaded text file (SHA: e07f5f5683ccc92...
2026-04-09T17:21:25.5055787Z 
2026-04-09T17:22:20.0382814Z Now I have enough information to assess the documentation gaps. Let me focus on the most impactful change.
2026-04-09T17:22:20.0383603Z 
2026-04-09T17:22:46.6699488Z The PR #57200 (by @m-nash) made a major architectural change: all Azure.Identity types were type-forwarded to Azure.Core. Despite a MigrationGuide.md being created, the Azure.Identity README has **no mention** of this consolidation — users reading it have no idea that Azure.Core 1.53+ already bundles all credential types. This is the largest gap.
2026-04-09T17:22:46.6701516Z

2026-04-09T17:23:01.1640974Z   - The Azure.Core README also doesn't explicitly call out that all Azure.Identity credential types are now included
2026-04-09T17:23:01.1641831Z   - The Azure.Identity README is missing a link to the migration guide and should clarify that new users might not need to install it separately anymore

2026-04-09T17:23:01.1653539Z ◆ Now I have a comprehensive picture. Let me analyze both changes:
2026-04-09T17:23:01.1653876Z   
2026-04-09T17:23:01.1654082Z   **Commit #58003 (needuv) - agentserver package:**
2026-04-09T17:23:01.1654633Z   - Changed `FoundryEnrichmentProcessor.OnStart` to read two OTel baggage keys and stamp them as span attributes:
2026-04-09T17:23:01.1655310Z     - `azure.ai.agentserver.session_id` → `microsoft.session.id`
2026-04-09T17:23:01.1655831Z     - `azure.ai.agentserver.conversation_id` → `gen_ai.conversation.id`
2026-04-09T17:23:01.1656711Z   - Updated `InvocationsActivitySource` to map `session_id` parameter to `microsoft.session.id` instead of `gen_ai.conversation.id`
2026-04-09T17:23:01.1657326Z   
2026-04-09T17:23:01.1657472Z   
2026-04-09T17:23:01.1657722Z   - Also treats empty or whitespace baggage values as absent
2026-04-09T17:23:01.1658038Z   
2026-04-09T17:23:01.1659118Z   The `README.md` for `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses` mentions OpenTelemetry tracing but doesn't detail the span attributes, and while the CHANGELOG notes the initial activity source release, it doesn't capture these new baggage enrichment changes from commit #58003.
2026-04-09T17:23:01.1660208Z   
2026-04-09T17:23:01.1662270Z   Looking at what's actually being added: the processor now reads `azure.ai.agentserver.session_id` and `azure.ai.agentserver.conversation_id` baggage values and maps them to `microsoft.session.id` and `gen_ai.conversation.id` span attributes respectively. The documentation gap is significant—there's no guidance on which baggage keys are supported, what attributes get emitted, how to pass session or conversation context, or how the `FoundryEnrichmentProcessor` works.
2026-04-09T17:23:01.1664021Z   
2026-04-09T17:23:01.1665106Z   Separately, commit #57200 consolidated Azure.Identity types into Azure.Core, adding a migration guide and updating both package changelogs, but the Azure.Identity README still presents itself as fully independent without mentioning this consolidation or the version cutoff where it took effect.
2026-04-09T17:23:01.1666250Z   
2026-04-09T17:23:01.1667557Z   Weighing these: the agentserver tracing gap is real but the package is still in beta, whereas the Azure.Identity/Azure.Core consolidation is a major architectural shift that could confuse users about which package to install—that's the more impactful documentation problem.
2026-04-09T17:23:01.1668620Z   
2026-04-09T17:23:01.1669261Z   But looking at what was actually committed, the most recent push is the agentserver changes, so I should focus there. The new baggage-bas... [truncated: showing first 2000 of 3339 chars]

This is the largest gap.

Session ID never goes\\ninto gen_ai.conversation.id. Both are set in OnStart because they are\\ncorrelation IDs that frameworks won't overwrite (unlike agent identity).\\n\\nAlso updates InvocationsActivitySource to map its session_id parameter to\\nmicrosoft.session.id instead of gen_ai.conversation.id.\\n\\nCo-authored-by: Copilot \\u003c223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com\\u003e\\n\\n* Treat empty/whitespace baggage values as absent\\n\\nUse string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace instead of null check when reading\\nsession_id and conversation_id from OTel baggage.
